event: Taira no Kiyomori sees the skulls of his victims
symbols: skulls piled in snow — the ghosts of the dead returning to haunt the living, the weight of violence
snow-covered landscape — the cold desolation of guilt and mortality
description: A Japanese woodblock triptych print showing a bald, robed elderly man (Taira no Kiyomori) standing on a veranda, gazing in horror at a landscape of snow that has transformed into a mass of human skulls. The skulls fill the left portion of the image, their pale forms merging with the snow. Behind the man, interior scenes with other figures are visible. Japanese text fills a cartouche in the upper right. The palette is dominated by whites, grays, purples, and muted tones with the ghostly skulls creating a supernatural atmosphere.

_meta.title: Taira no Kiyomori sees the skulls of his victims
